headline: Pick berries and feed tomatoes before noon storms.
weather_notes: Finish all outdoor garden work before noon — today's 51% afternoon storm chance closes the outdoor window; mornings are clear and manageable at 95°F vs last week's 100°F+ heat.
Skip zone irrigation today — both zones ran Jul 16; hold to see if this afternoon's storms deliver meaningful rain before scheduling the next cycle.
Inspect e1–e4 after any afternoon storm — check 27-day transplants for pooling or stem lodging from heavy monsoon downpours.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 7 days overdue; this morning before storms is the cleanest outdoor window of the week — get it done before Saturday's 37% storm pattern repeats.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest blackberries before noon
suggested_tasks.description: Today's 51% afternoon storm chance risks bruising ripe Triple Crown fruit on the cane; pick this morning before storms arrive.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed e1–e4 tomatoes — fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 27-day transplants have no logged fertilizer; apply this morning so today's afternoon storms help wash it into the root zone.
